fact: July 14 is exactly 195 days into the year, which means there are exactly 170 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat season - the sun stays up for over 15 hours in many places, making it perfect for epic water balloon fights!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest around this time of year in North America, creating a buzzing symphony that can reach 120 decibels - louder than a chainsaw!
July 14 falls smack in the middle of Cancer season in astrology, when the moon supposedly makes people extra emotional and crabby (just like the Cancer crab symbol).
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, July 14 is deep winter - penguins are huddling together for warmth while kids up north are complaining it's too hot!
Fireflies are at their flashiest peak around mid-July in many regions, blinking their butts off to find mates in the warm summer nights.
This date sits almost exactly one month after the summer solstice, meaning daylight is just starting to get shorter each day - but you probably won't notice for weeks!
July 14 is prime time for thunderstorms in many parts of the world because hot air rises faster, creating those dramatic towering clouds that look like giant cauliflower.
Mosquitoes are at their most annoying around this time of year because the hot, humid weather makes them breed like crazy in any standing water they can find.
In northern regions like Alaska, the sun barely sets at all around July 14 - it just dips toward the horizon and bounces back up, creating an endless golden hour.
Many flowers are going completely bonkers with growth right now - sunflowers can grow up to 6 inches in a single week during peak summer!
Ocean temperatures in the Northern Hemisphere are finally catching up to air temperatures around July 14, making it perfect timing for swimming without turning into a popsicle.
